name: Rokhlin's Multiple Mixing Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the ergodic system (X, ℬ, μ, T) — a probability space with a measure-preserving automorphism T

VINIT.justification: Before any mixing property is asserted, there is no information about correlations between past and future. The void state represents the uninitialized system before any temporal structure is imposed.

TANCH.chosen_element: the full σ-algebra ℬ (all measurable sets)
TANCH.justification: The complete σ-algebra is the hermetic boundary that contains all possible events and all mixing information. It is the terminal anchor — the closed system within which all mixing occurs.

AFWD.justification: T^n is the directed morphism pushing the system forward in time. It is the forward transformation toward the target state (future mixing).

AREV.justification: T^{-n} is the contravariant reverse morphism, descending back through time. It represents the inverse transformation and the descent into past correlations.

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of mixing orders: T^{n_1} ∘ T^{n_2} ∘ ... ∘ T^{n_k}
CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of time steps. Multiple mixing conditions are composed together to form the full hierarchy of mixing orders (weak, strong, multiple).

IMSCRIB.justification: The identity is self-reference: the system recognizing itself at a single moment. It is the element that is itself, unchanged by time.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the branching into k-fold mixing: the system splits into k independent temporal channels
FSPLIT.justification: Rokhlin's theorem asserts that a mixing transformation can be decomposed into k independent mixing subsystems. FSPLIT represents the decomposition of the single transformation T into k parallel mixing processes, each with its own temporal evolution.

FFUSE.chosen_element: the reconstitution of the original transformation from k-fold mixing
FFUSE.justification: After k independent mixing channels evolve, they must fuse back into the original T. The Frobenius constraint FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T holds: the k-fold mixing, when recombined, recovers the original measure-preserving transformation exactly.

EVALT.chosen_element: the affirmative mixing condition: lim_{n→∞} |μ(A ∩ T^{-n}B) - μ(A)μ(B)| = 0
EVALT.justification: This is the true/affirmative branch: the mixing property holds. Future and past become independent as time goes to infinity.

EVALF.chosen_element: the failure of mixing: lim_{n→∞} |μ(A ∩ T^{-n}B) - μ(A)μ(B)| ≠ 0
EVALF.justification: This is the false/negative branch: the mixing property fails. Correlations persist; the system does not forget its past.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the paradice of multiple mixing orders: simultaneously weak-mixing and strong-mixing
ENGAGR.justification: Rokhlin's theorem reveals a paradice: a transformation can be both weakly mixing (in the sense of L^2 convergence) and strongly mixing (in the sense of pointwise convergence of correlations). Both conditions hold without resolution into a single hierarchy.

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent record of the mixing spectrum: the eigenvalues {λ_j} of the Koopman operator U_T
IFIX.justification: The spectrum is append-only and irreversible. Once the spectral decomposition is computed, it is fixed and cannot be undone. It is the permanent witness to the mixing structure.

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: decomposition into k independent mixing subsystems
split_outputs: T_1: the first mixing channel (e.g., weak-mixing component)
T_2: the second mixing channel (e.g., strong-mixing component)
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: reconstitution of T from k-fold mixing
phase_2.fuse_result: the original transformation T = T_1 ∘ T_2 ∘ ... ∘ T_k (up to measure-theoretic equivalence)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — the uninitialized system before any temporal structure or mixing property is defined. No σ-algebra, no measure, no automorphism.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — the affirmative state: the mixing property holds. Correlations decay to zero; the system forgets its past. Forward evolution succeeds.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — the negative state: the mixing property fails. Correlations persist; the system retains memory. Backward descent reveals non-trivial structure.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — the paradice: the system is simultaneously weakly mixing and strongly mixing. Both mixing orders hold without resolution. The spectral decomposition exhibits both discrete and continuous components.

steps.domain_action: initialize the empty σ-algebra ∅ — the void state before any mixing structure exists
establish the full σ-algebra ℬ as the boundary containing all measurable events
apply forward time evolution T^n (n > 0) — push the system toward the future
compose multiple time steps T^{n_1} ∘ T^{n_2} — chain temporal evolution
recognize the identity T^0 = id — the system at a single moment, unchanged
decompose T into k independent mixing channels — branch into T_1, T_2, ..., T_k
affirm the mixing condition on the T-branch — lim_{n→∞} |μ(A ∩ T^{-n}B) - μ(A)μ(B)| = 0
forward evolution within the T-branch — correlations decay as n → ∞
record the spectrum of the Koopman operator U_T on the T-branch — permanent witness to mixing
backward time evolution T^{-n} on the F-branch — descend into past correlations
negate the mixing condition on the F-branch — lim_{n→∞} |μ(A ∩ T^{-n}B) - μ(A)μ(B)| ≠ 0
record the non-trivial eigenvalues on the F-branch — permanent witness to non-mixing
hold both mixing orders simultaneously — weak-mixing and strong-mixing coexist without resolution
compose the k-fold mixing channels — T_1 ∘ T_2 ∘ ... ∘ T_k
reconstitute the original transformation T from k-fold mixing — fuse T_1, T_2, ..., T_k back into T
recognize that FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T — the system is self-referential, closed under decomposition and reconstitution
close the system within the full σ-algebra ℬ — all mixing information is contained
record the final spectral decomposition — the permanent, irreversible witness to Rokhlin's multiple mixing theorem

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the ergodic-theoretic proof engine: translates the measure-preserving property and mixing conditions into spectral decompositions and Koopman operator eigenvalues
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the σ-algebra communication protocol: measurable sets exchange information via the measure μ; time evolution T propagates correlations between past and future events
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the Koopman operator spectrum: eigenvalues {λ_j} and eigenfunctions {φ_j} store the mixing structure; the spectral measure records the distribution of mixing rates
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the time evolution T^n: determines the order and timing of correlation decay; n → ∞ schedules the asymptotic mixing behavior
phase_5.alfs_store: the canonical reference: Rokhlin's original theorem (1949) and its extensions; the spectral theorem for unitary operators on L^2(X, μ)

phase_6.cycle_cost: the information loss per time step: H(A | T^{-n}B) → 0 as n → ∞ (mixing dissipates correlations)
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: the system state before mixing: A and T^{-n}B are correlated; μ(A ∩ T^{-n}B) ≠ μ(A)μ(B)
phase_6.post_cycle_state: the system state after mixing: A and T^{-n}B are independent; μ(A ∩ T^{-n}B) ≈ μ(A)μ(B)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative because the measure μ is preserved by T (measure-preserving automorphism). The total entropy of the system remains constant; mixing redistributes entropy uniformly across the phase space without creating or destroying information.

-- Class: Rokhlin's Multiple Mixing Theorem

  -- Seq chain:

  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (apply forward time evolution T^n (n > 0) — push the system toward the future)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose multiple time steps T^{n_1} ∘ T^{n_2} — chain temporal evolution)

        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (backward time evolution T^{-n} on the F-branch — descend into past correlations)
      -- F-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (negate the mixing condition on the F-branch — lim_{n→∞} |μ(A ∩ T^{-n}B) - μ(A...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the non-trivial eigenvalues on the F-branch — permanent witness to non...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (hold both mixing orders simultaneously — weak-mixing and strong-mixing coexis...)
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [13] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the k-fold mixing channels — T_1 ∘ T_2 ∘ ... ∘ T_k))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [14]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [14] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute the original transformation T from k-fold mixing — fuse T_1, T_2, ..., T_k back into T)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [15] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (recognize that FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T — the system is self-referential, closed ...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [16] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (close the system within the full σ-algebra ℬ — all mixing information is cont...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑡 𐑼)  -- [17] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the final spectral decomposition — the permanent, irreversible witness...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def rokhlins_multiple_mixing_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (rokhlins_multiple_mixing_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm

  (rokhlins_multiple_mixing_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF
